headline: Esports World Cup 2026 Hits Max Level in Week 3: Big Money, Big Matches, and a Heartfelt Tribute
dek: From a $75 million prize pool to surging viewership and a newly named trophy honoring a beloved legend, the EWC is delivering on all fronts.
body_markdown:
The Esports World Cup 2026 has fully hit its stride, entering its third week of high-octane competition in Paris, France. Running from July 20-26, this year's event is shattering records with a colossal $75 million prize pool, setting a new benchmark for esports tournaments.

Week 3 is a packed arena, showcasing five premier competitions that span multiple genres. Fans are currently watching the FC Pro World Championship for EA SPORTS FC, the intense battles of PUBG: BATTLEGROUNDS, the strategic clashes of Mobile Legends: Bang Bang (MLBB) Mid Season Cup, the precise combat of Street Fighter 6, and the tactical depth of Teamfight Tactics.

A particularly touching moment this week centers around the FC Pro World Championship. The prestigious trophy has been renamed the Diogo Jota FC Pro World Championship Trophy, a poignant tribute to the late Liverpool and Portugal forward. Jota's family granted their blessing for the renaming, honoring his deep and lifelong passion for EA SPORTS FC and esports. Adding to the football fanfare, legend Karim Benzema is slated to participate in a special one-off showmatch during the FC Pro Finals weekend.

The energy in Paris is palpable, and the viewership numbers reflect it. During its second week, the EWC accumulated over 47.8 million Hours Watched. This impressive figure represents double the engagement from the opening week and a 34.4% increase over the equivalent period in EWC 2025. League of Legends led the charge, peaking at over 1.48 million viewers, marking the highest viewership for any EWC event this year. Free Fire also saw a significant surge, peaking at over 804K viewers and achieving its most popular event in four years.

Fans of the FC Pro tournament can catch the League Stage running until July 24, with the knockout phase and single-elimination playoffs scheduled for July 25-26. The PUBG: BATTLEGROUNDS competition concluded its Group Stage, which ran from July 21-23, with the top 16 teams now advancing to the Grand Finals, which will unfold from July 24-26. Meanwhile, the MLBB Mid Season Cup is deep into its Group Stage, running from July 22-26, as 16 teams battle it out in a double-elimination format.
